title: React Components – Building Reusable UI Pieces

meta.title: React Components – Complete Guide to Component Patterns (2026)
meta.description: Master React components - functional, class, pure, and higher-order components. Learn component patterns, composition, and reusability.

content.value: Components are the building blocks of React applications. They encapsulate UI logic and rendering, making code reusable and maintainable. Components accept inputs called props and return React elements describing what should appear on screen.

value: **Single responsibility** – Each component should do one thing well
**Keep components small** – Aim for < 200 lines per component
**Use meaningful names** – Component names should describe what they do
**Props should be minimal** – Only pass what the component needs
**Use composition over inheritance** – React has a powerful composition model
**Extract reusable logic** – Use custom hooks instead of HOCs
**Memoize when necessary** – Use React.memo for expensive components
**Keep components pure** – Same props should produce same output
**Colocate related files** – Keep styles, tests with components
**Document components** – Use PropTypes, TypeScript, or comments

content.value: React components are powerful building blocks for UI development. Start with functional components and hooks for new projects. Use composition to build complex UIs from simple pieces. Choose patterns like HOCs, render props, or custom hooks based on your specific needs.

faq.question: Functional vs class components?
faq.answer: Functional components with hooks are recommended for new code. Class components are legacy but still work.

faq.question: What is React.memo?
faq.answer: A higher-order component that memoizes functional components to prevent unnecessary re-renders.

faq.question: What is a higher-order component?
faq.answer: A function that takes a component and returns a new component with enhanced functionality.

faq.question: When to use custom hooks vs HOCs?
faq.answer: Custom hooks are simpler for sharing logic; HOCs are better for augmenting components with additional UI.

quiz.question: Which component type is recommended for new React code?
What is a higher-order component?
options: A component that renders other components
A function that returns a component
